behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real STT diarization quality: the behavior explicitly requires STT output to "preserve speaker labels." A stubbed transcript would hide label-loss and misattribution failures. This is not Workflow because the assertion is about one model step’s output quality, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run transcription on real meeting audio through the real speech-to-text API and assert speaker labels are present and consistently attached to utterances used by downstream attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/structuring logic (assignee + task text extraction) and can fail entirely within one local parser boundary with fixed transcript text. No real OS/network/subprocess or model judgment is required.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixture transcripts containing explicit assignment phrases and assert extracted objects include correct assignee and task text fields in the summary input structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM content decisions (coverage/completeness per attendee). Deterministic mocks can always return compliant output and miss omission failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior does not require validating multiple agentic handoff decisions, only final summary quality against assignment coverage.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript inputs with known per-attendee task assignments, call the real summarization model, and verify each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in the live output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text targets routing logic after decisions are already identified. That is a local transformation/schema-mapping boundary and does not require real infra or real model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Provide an input object with pre-identified decisions and assert mapper output places them only in `decisions` schema fields, not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks model extraction/placement quality from raw transcript content. A mock response would hide real misses where decisions are omitted or placed narratively. Rejected Workflow: requirement is one summary output quality check, not multi-agentic-stage sequencing correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Send real decision-rich transcripts to the real summarization model and assert expected decisions are present in the `decisions` section and absent as only narrative mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequential segment processing plus merged coherence, which chains multiple agentic outputs (per-segment transcription/summarization then merge). Single-step Agentic is too low because handoff across multiple model-produced segment results is the core failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real long-audio pipeline (>60 min) end-to-end with real model calls, verify segmenting and sequential execution occurred, then validate merged summary coherence and no dropped segment content.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an explicit full pipeline success criterion over real recording input, including multiple agentic stages (transcription + summarization) and their orchestration. Lower levels cannot expose cross-stage automation failures that appear only in full chained execution.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like run with a real meeting audio file: upload, real STT call, real LLM summary generation, and delivery of structured output; assert unattended completion and schema-valid final artifact.
